findings.title: Failed trip DELETE silently swallowed — trip vanishes then reappears on next poll with no error
findings.rootCause: deleteTrip() -> _delete() (core.ts:302) calls apiFetch(...).catch(e=>console.error). apiFetch only THROWS on network-level failures (it returns the Response for any HTTP status, including 4xx/5xx). So a 500/403 is a resolved promise with res.ok=false. Neither deleteActiveTrip (trip-controls.ts:200-204) nor deleteArchivedTrip (handlers.ts:135-139) ever inspects res.ok — they only `catch` (which never fires for an HTTP error) and then navigate. Meanwhile delete_trip server-side only writes the trip_deletes tombstone INSIDE the transaction (trips.py:443), so a failed delete leaves no tombstone and the row persists. pullFromServer does `STATE.trips = allTrips.filter(...)` (api.ts:183) — a full replace from the server — so the still-existing trip is re-added. The optimistic local removal is undone with zero user feedback.

findings.title: Failed trip CREATE (daily cap 429 / 500) is silently swallowed; new trip is lost on next poll
findings.rootCause: openNewTripModal’s submit does `try { await upsertTrip(newTrip); await Promise.all(scaffolded.map(upsertDay)) } catch { /* outbox retries */ } navigate('home')`. upsertTrip -> _upsertWithUpdatedAt returns {ok:false,status:429|500,...} WITHOUT throwing for any HTTP response (api.ts:419 returns the envelope; only network errors throw). So the catch never fires and the return value is ignored. The outbox is only populated in apiFetch’s network-failure catch (core.ts:236-256), so a 429/500 is NOT queued for retry. On the next pull, STATE.trips is fully replaced from the server (api.ts:183), which has no such trip → it vanishes. Note the AI-plan flow explicitly handles userCapHit (useAiPlan.ts:370) but the trip-create flow does not.

findings.title: Cloned trip's marked places keep the SOURCE trip's dayId → wishlist pins become invisible on every day filter
findings.rootCause: _clone_trip_attempt copies src['marked_places_json'] VERBATIM (trips.py:1309) while generating brand-new day ids for the clone’s trip_days. So each cloned marked place still carries dayId pointing at a SOURCE-trip day that does not exist in the clone. In todoMarkers.ts the day filter does: if (!selectedIsAnchor && place.dayId && place.dayId !== selectedDayId) continue (line 183) and if (!selectedIsAnchor && !place.dayId) continue (line 186). A place with a dangling dayId never equals any real day in the clone, so it’s skipped on every numbered-day view, AND because it HAS a dayId it’s skipped on the Anchor view too → invisible everywhere. The template snapshot path explicitly strips dayId/timeOfDay for exactly this reason (templates.py:106-107); the clone path never got the same treatment.

findings.title: No cap on trip date range → unbounded day scaffolding; days beyond 999 silently rejected by the server
findings.rootCause: mountDateRangePicker configures flatpickr in range mode with NO maxDate and no max-span (dateRangePicker.ts:89-101). _scaffoldTripDays loops day-by-day from start to end with no upper bound (_shared.ts:109). The create handler then does Promise.all(scaffolded.map(upsertDay)) (trip.ts:229) with no batching and no error inspection. Server-side upsert_day rejects dayNumber>999 with HTTP 400 (days.py:72-73), but upsertDay’s {ok:false} is ignored, so days 1000..N silently fail while 1..999 persist. The local STATE keeps all N days, diverging from the server.

findings.title: Maps-unavailable country fallback creates the Day-0 anchor at lat/lng 0,0 (Gulf of Guinea)
findings.rootCause: The Maps-down fallback in _wirePlacePicker builds the picked place with lat:0,lng:0 (and viewport:null) because it only resolves a country code, not coordinates (_shared.ts:217-218). The create modal copies these straight onto the trip (trip.ts:184-185). The lazy Day-0 creator gates only on `typeof activeTrip.lat === 'number'` (handlers.ts:243-244) — and 0 IS a number — so it creates the anchor day with lat:0,lng:0 (handlers.ts:252-253). Nothing treats (0,0) as 'no pin yet' on the anchor path (todoMarkers.ts:181 special-cases 0,0 for markers, but the anchor day creation does not).

findings.title: Background /api/data poll replaces STATE.trips while the Edit-Trip modal is open, detaching the edited object
findings.rootCause: openEditTripModal captures a reference to a specific STATE.trips object (trip.ts:243). pullFromServer unconditionally does `STATE.trips = allTrips.filter(...)` (api.ts:183) — building brand-new objects — even when a modal is open; the modal-open guard at api.ts:335-336 only suppresses the re-NAVIGATE, not the STATE replacement. So mid-edit the modal’s `trip` becomes an orphan no longer in STATE. On submit, the modal mutates the detached object and emits state:changed (which re-renders from the fresh STATE objects, momentarily dropping the user’s edits), then upsertTrip(detached) sends the change with the updatedAt captured at modal-open. If anyone touched the trip server-side in between, that stamp is stale → 409 staleEdit toast (api.ts:413) for a single-editor user.

findings.title: 'Day deleted' success toast is a hardcoded English string (i18n gap)
findings.rootCause: deleteDay’s onConfirm calls showLiquidAlert('Day deleted') with a literal string instead of t(...) (home-mount/handlers.ts:321). Every other toast in this flow uses t(); this one was missed. The confirm dialog itself (errors.deleteDayTitle/Body/ConfirmBtn) is correctly localized, so only the success toast is wrong.

findings.title: Editing trip dates with all-undated existing days silently no-ops the date change
findings.rootCause: In the edit-dates branch, oldFirst/oldLast are parsed from numberedDays[0].date / [last].date (trip.ts:527-528). When those dates are empty, parseUTC('') returns null, so startChanged and endChanged (trip.ts:532-535) are both false (they require oldFirst/oldLast to be non-null). The rebase (line 539) and extend/shorten (line 565) blocks are gated on startChanged/endChanged, so nothing runs — the picked dates are written only to trip.dateFrom/dateTo (finalizeAndClose) but never propagated to the day rows. The 'numberedDays.length === 0' scaffold path (line 518) doesn’t apply because numbered days exist (just dateless).
